text.title: Tool Definition and tool_choice
text.summary: Claude tools require a name, description, and JSON Schema input_schema; tool_choice controls whether Claude must call a tool and which one.

rule.statement: Write tool descriptions as if explaining to a colleague who has never seen your codebase — specificity prevents wrong tool selection; vague descriptions lead to wrong tool calls with no error.
rule.rationale: Tool descriptions are the primary signal for tool selection. The model has no other context for choosing between tools.

antipattern.description: Tool schemas with more than 3 levels of nesting.
antipattern.reason: Deep nesting increases tool-call errors. Keep input schemas flat (2 levels max) for highest reliability.

list.item: auto (default): model decides whether to use a tool and which one
any: model must call at least one tool, picks which
tool + name: model must call the specified tool — use for forced structured output

example.code: # Force a specific tool for structured output extraction message = client.messages.create( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, tools=[json_tool], tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "output_json"}, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Extract: John Doe, 30, john@example.com"}] ) tool_use = next(b for b in message.content if b.type == "tool_use") data = tool_use.input # typed dict matching the schema

rule.statement: Check stop_reason == "tool_use" explicitly on every response; missing this check causes the agent loop to exit early on tool requests.
rule.rationale: tool_use is a stop, not an error. The API returns stop_reason="tool_use" whenever Claude wants to call a tool; the caller must handle it.
